question: What's the best time to visit Orange NSW with kids?
answer: G'day! Orange is brilliant year-round, but autumn (March-May) is absolutely stunning with the leaves changing colour. Spring (September-November) brings gorgeous blossoms and mild weather perfect for little ones. Winter can be chilly but magical if you love cooler temps. School holidays get busy, so book early! Summer's great too, though it can get warm during the day—perfect for splash parks and outdoor fun.

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Orange NSW?
answer: You bet! Several holiday parks around Orange welcome your furry family members. Most pet-friendly parks require dogs to be on-leash and have designated areas. You'll typically need to bring your pet's bedding and clean up after them. Always check specific pet policies when booking, as some cabins are pet-friendly while others aren't. Fees usually apply, around $10-20 per night per pet.

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Orange NSW during school holidays?
answer: School holiday prices vary quite a bit! Expect to pay around $150-280 per night for a family cabin during peak times. Powered caravan sites are more budget-friendly at $45-70 per night. Off-peak rates can be 20-30% cheaper. Easter and summer holidays are priciest. Booking a few months ahead saves you dollars and guarantees availability. Many parks offer multi-night discounts too!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Pack layers—Orange weather can be unpredictable! Bring comfy clothes, gumboots for puddle jumping, sunscreen, hats, and insect repellent. Don't forget favourite toys, books, and comfort items. Most cabins have kitchens, so bring basics if self-catering. Pack a first-aid kit, torches for night adventures, and outdoor gear like bikes or scooters. Baby monitors are handy if your cabin has separate bedrooms!

question: What is there to do near Orange NSW holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps of fun awaits! Visit the excellent playgrounds at Cook Park and Robertson Park. Lake Canobolas is perfect for picnics and nature walks. Kids love the Orange Adventure Playground and Bloomfield Science Museum. During autumn, don't miss apple picking at local orchards. The Botanic Gardens are gorgeous for exploring. Orange Aquatic Centre keeps kids entertained rain or shine. Plenty of family-friendly cafes and restaurants nearby too!
